blocks.text: Alternative Education Programme About the Programme Rosehill College leads the Alternative Education (Alt Ed) Programme for the Papakura area, in partnership with Blue Light Papakura and Te Awatea. The programme provides flexible, supportive learning pathways for students in Years 9–11 (under 16 years old) who need a tailored environment to succeed. The purpose of the programme is to provide new learning opportunities for students who have become disengaged from mainstream education. Who is this Programme for? This programme is designed for students who have become disengaged from mainstream education and would benefit from a more personalised and supportive learning environment. Enrolment Process 1. Referral or Enquiry Contact the Alt Ed team or speak with your Kaitiaki. 2. Submit Enrolment Form Complete and return the Alternative Education Enrolment Form 3. Initial Interview Student, whānau, and referring school meet with the Alt Ed team 4. Transition Plan A personalised learning and attendance plan is developed Contact Information Enrolments Email: alted@rosehillcollege.school.nz Phone: (09) 295 0661 ext 871 Lead Support Mr J Fraser Email: j.fraser@rosehillcollege.school.nz Programme Focus Core Learning: Literacy, numeracy, and NCEA Foundation Level Pathways: Transition planning, employment skills, and work readiness Life Skills: Hauora, resilience, communication, and teamwork Service & Leadership: Building social responsibility and whanaungatanga Support: Small class sizes, mentoring, and wrap-around pastoral care Programme Options (2026) Rosehill College (On Site) Focus: Re-engagement, foundation learning, transition to school Blue Light Papakura Focus: Youth development and leadership Papakura Activity Centre (Te Awatea) Focus: Holistic, whānau-based learning and wellbeing support ‍ Offsite Programme Offers Pick-ups and drop-offs Breakfast and lunch provided Access to facilities including: Fitness centre Music recording Outdoor education Expected Outcomes The programme aims to support students to: Re-engage in education Improve attendance and engagement Develop literacy, numeracy, and communication skills Build confidence, resilience, and self-motivation Transition into further education, training, or employment Apply Now To apply, please complete the Alternative Education Enrolment Form and return it to: alted@rosehillcollege.school.nz DOWNLOAD
Rosehill College leads the Alternative Education (Alt Ed) Programme for the Papakura area, in partnership with Blue Light Papakura and Te Awatea.
The programme provides flexible, supportive learning pathways for students in Years 9–11 (under 16 years old) who need a tailored environment to succeed.
The purpose of the programme is to provide new learning opportunities for students who have become disengaged from mainstream education.
This programme is designed for students who have become disengaged from mainstream education and would benefit from a more personalised and supportive learning environment.
Contact the Alt Ed team or speak with your Kaitiaki.
Complete and return the Alternative Education Enrolment Form
Student, whānau, and referring school meet with the Alt Ed team
A personalised learning and attendance plan is developed
Core Learning: Literacy, numeracy, and NCEA Foundation Level
Pathways: Transition planning, employment skills, and work readiness
Life Skills: Hauora, resilience, communication, and teamwork
Service & Leadership: Building social responsibility and whanaungatanga
Support: Small class sizes, mentoring, and wrap-around pastoral care
Focus: Re-engagement, foundation learning, transition to school
Focus: Holistic, whānau-based learning and wellbeing support
Access to facilities including: Fitness centre Music recording Outdoor education
The programme aims to support students to:
Develop literacy, numeracy, and communication skills
Transition into further education, training, or employment
To apply, please complete the Alternative Education Enrolment Form and return it to:
